audioscript.text: Section 1, you will hear a telephone conversation between a woman and a police officer.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First look at questions 1 to 5.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: For each of the questions, four alternatives are given.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Decide which of the alternatives a, b, c or d.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Best fits what you hear on the tape, and circle the appropriate letter.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will see that there is an example which has been done for you.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On this occasion only, the conversation relating to the example will be played first.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good evening, City Police Station.
audioscript.speaker: Receptionist

audioscript.text: Can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: Receptionist

audioscript.text: Oh, hello.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: I'd like to report a stolen briefcase, please.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Just a minute, and I'll put you through.
audioscript.speaker: Receptionist

audioscript.text: The woman says she wants to report a stolen briefcase, so a has been circled.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now we shall begin.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You should answer the questions as you listen, because you will not hear the recording a second time.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully to the first part of the conversation, and answer questions 1 to 5.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good evening, City Police Station.
audioscript.speaker: Receptionist

audioscript.text: Can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: Receptionist

audioscript.text: Oh, hello.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: I'd like to report a stolen briefcase, please.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Just a minute, and I'll put you through.
audioscript.speaker: Receptionist

audioscript.text: Lost property, can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Oh, yes.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: I've had my briefcase stolen.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: OK, I'll take some details.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Tell me what it looks like first of all.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Well, it's a soft leather one, you know, not a heavy box type like a man's.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Hmm. And how does it close?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: It's got buckles at the front, two of them, they're gold plated ones.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Fine.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Uh. Was it locked?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: No, I'm afraid not.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Never mind.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Any distinguishing features?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Pardon?
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Any marks or badges on it that make it stand out?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Uh. Only the brand name.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: And where's that?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: It's on the back, at the bottom, in the left-hand corner.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: It's Sagi.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Oh, and there's a scratch.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: It's quite bad, but small, directly above the brand name.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: I did it recently putting it on my bike.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Right, got that.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: So, what did you have inside the briefcase?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Well, all my papers from college.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: It's so frustrating, but thank goodness for computers, I haven't lost them completely.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Yes, you're lucky.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: I had my wallet in my pocket, so I didn't lose that.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: But there were also my pens which I got for my birthday, and a novel I was planning to read on the train.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Right, where exactly did you lose the briefcase?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Well, I couldn't believe it.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: I was standing on the platform, it was right next to me.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: You were holding it?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: I just put it down on the floor, but I could almost feel it beside me.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: I was watching for my tram because sometimes it comes early, and then next time I looked, my briefcase wasn't there.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: And what time was this?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: It was it must have been about 5:20 no, a bit later.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: I'd say 5:30, because it was just getting crowded, and the train normally comes at about 5:35 (twenty-five to six).
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Before they continue their conversation, look at questions 6 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: As you listen to the rest of the conversation, complete the form by filling in the numbered spaces 6 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Right, if you'll just give me some personal details.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Yes.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: What name is it?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: I'm Mary Prescott.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Can you spell that?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Yes, it's PRESCOTT.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: And your address?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Flat 2, 41, Fountain Road, Canterbury.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Fountain Road, yes, No. 41.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: And have you got a contact telephone number?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Yes, it's 752239.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Fine.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Uh. One last question, what would you say the value of your briefcase is?
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Including the contents?
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Yes.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Just a rough estimate is fine.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Oh, I'm not sure.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Well, the briefcase itself is quite new.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: I bought it last month for £40 I suppose about 65 pounds.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: The contents are worth about 20 or 25 pounds at least.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: That's fine.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: Well, um, if you could come down to the station tomorrow, you can sign this form and have a look at what we've got here.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: OK, thanks.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Bye.
audioscript.speaker: Woman

audioscript.text: Goodbye.
audioscript.speaker: Police Officer

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Section 2, now listen to the news headlines, and tick the three other items which are mentioned.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: This is the 6 o'clock news for Tuesday the 25th of November. And first the headlines.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: The Prime Minister has promised to help the drought stricken farmers in the northern part of the country who haven't seen rain for nearly two years.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: And in Sydney, a group of school children are successfully rescued from a plane which landed in the sea shortly after takeoff.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: Transport workers are on strike in Melbourne, over a pay claim, and the strike looks set to spread to other states.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: And on a fashionable note, there's to be a new look for the staff of QANTAS, Australia's national airline.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: Now you have some time to look at questions 14 to 21.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: As you listen to the rest of the news, complete the notes in the spaces provided.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: The Prime Minister has pledged today that he will make $250 million available to help the drought stricken farmers who have not seen rain for years get through the next five years.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: Money that was to have been spent on the restructuring of Sydney's road system has been reallocated to what the Prime Minister described as 'a more worthy cause'.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: Farmers are to receive financial assistance to help see them through the worst drought in over 50 years.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: Many farmers feel that while the money is welcome, it has come too late to save them and their farms from financial ruin, and are angry that the government did not act sooner.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: A group of school children who were traveling in a privately chartered airplane from Sydney to Queensland to take part in a musical concert found themselves swimming for the shore when their airplane had to land in the sea, just three minutes after taking off from Sydney airport.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: The pilot managed to bring the aircraft and its 50 passengers down safely in the calm waters of Botany Bay, where boats and pleasure craft were able to come to the rescue of the boys.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: The fact that it was a weekend meant that there were hundreds of boats in the bay enjoying the good weather, and this undoubtedly helped the rescue operation.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: 'We owe our lives to the skill of the pilot,' said one of the boys,
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: but the pilot replied modestly that it was 'all part of a day's work'.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: However, all their musical instruments were lost, and they never got to play at the concert.
audioscript.speaker: Newsreader

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

body.title: Read the following Example and Tick the THREE other items which are mentioned in the news headlines. (Example) [✓] Money promised for drought victims.
options: Bus and train drivers national strike threat

items: The Government plans to give $ <input> to assist the farmers. This money was to be spent on improving Sydney's <input> but has now been re-allocated. Australia has experienced its worst drought in over fifty years. Farmers say that the money will not help them because it is <input>.

An aeroplane which was carrying a group of <input> was forced to land just <input> minutes after take-off. The passengers were rescued by <input>. The operation was helped because of the good weather. The passengers thanked the <input> for saving their lives but unfortunately they lost their <input>.

answer_key.correct_answer: boats | pleasure craft | boats and pleasure craft

test_text.title: Conversation between a New Student and a Female Lecturer

audioscript.text: Section 3, in this section, you will hear a conversation between a university student and a university lecturer.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Look at questions 22 to 25 for each of the questions, four alternatives are given.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Decide which of the alternatives best fits what you hear on the tape.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: And circle the appropriate letter.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Hello, uh, can I come in?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Oh, yes, come in.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: How can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: I was looking for the economics office.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: I've been all over the Arts Faculty building looking for it.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: But I could only find the school of accounting and economic history.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Is this the right place?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Yes, this is the School of Economics.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Oh, good.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Um. I'm a new student, and I was wondering if someone could give me some information.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Well, I might be able to help.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: I lecture on that program.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: What do you need to know?
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Oh, quite a few things actually.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Firstly, how many lectures a week do I have to attend?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Oh, well, the Economics 1 course is a double unit, so there are two lectures a week and one tutorial.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Oh, the lectures are scheduled for Tuesday and Thursday.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: What time?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Uh. Let me see.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Um. You know this information is all in the handout which you should have received yesterday at the orientation meeting.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Uh. Oh, was there was there a meeting yesterday?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: I didn't know about that.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Um. No one yes, ha ha, there was, but never mind.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Now the lectures are at 4 in the afternoon.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Oh, four's a bit late.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: I've got a part-time job that starts at 4:30.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Well, you can't be in two places at once, can you?
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: And attendance at lectures is necessary.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: We expect at least 90% attendance at this university, you know?
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: 90 percent, that's high.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Do they enforce that rule?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Yes we do.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: We're pretty strict about it actually.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: And what times have been set down for the tutorials?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Do you have that information?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: That's a very well attended course, so there's a number of tutorial times, um, Monday, Wednesday and Friday, all at 9:00.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Yours will be allocated at the first lecture.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Can't I choose the time?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Maybe maybe not, you'll have to talk to the lecturer on the course.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Dr. Roberts is his name.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Oh.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: OK.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Now look at questions 26 to 31.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: As the conversation continues, complete the notes in the spaces provided.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Anything else I can help you with while you're here?
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Well, yes, actually.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Do you know what the course requirements are?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: I mean, how much work is expected for this course?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Well, you have to complete a tutorial paper.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: What does that involve?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Well, it's a piece of work on a given topic, based on some set reading texts.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: You'll have to give a small talk to your tutorial group.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Hmm, how long does that have to be?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Oh, about 25 minutes usually.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: I have to talk for 25 minutes.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Yes, that's right.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: And then you have to write up your piece of work, and give it to the lecturer to be marked.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Right, uh, and is that all?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: No, you also have to complete a 3,000 word essay on a topic.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Can I choose the topic?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Yes, usually you can.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Right, huh, that shouldn't be too bad.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: And in addition to that, there is an exam.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: An exam!
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: What sort of exam?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Well, it's an open book exam.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Does that mean I can have the textbook with me during the exam?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Yes, that's right.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: And can you give me any idea about the content of the first year of economics, so that I can get into some reading?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Well, you'll be getting the reading list next week when lectures start.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: All the books are in the library.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Yes, but won't everyone else take them out as soon as they get the reading list too?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Well, yes, they might. But most of the important ones are held in Closed Reserve.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: That's a part of the library where you can go to read books, but you can't take them out of the building.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: What did you call that section of the library?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Closed Reserve.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: However, we do recommend that you buy the core books.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: You'll find them useful, and you'll need them for the exam.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Yes, I suppose I will.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: But what is the focus of the course?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Well, the course at this university has a vocational focus.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: That is a focus on preparing its graduates for work, so we're oriented very much towards employment.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Oh, so my chances of getting a job are good?
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Well, provided you get good results.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: Well look, thanks for your time.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: You've been really helpful.
audioscript.speaker: male student

audioscript.text: Ha ha, that's fine.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: See you next week then.
audioscript.speaker: female lecturer

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Look at questions 22 to 25 for each of the questions, four alternatives are given.

utterances.text: Hello, uh, can I come in?
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Oh, yes, come in.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: How can I help you?
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: I was looking for the economics office.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: I've been all over the Arts Faculty building looking for it.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: But I could only find the school of accounting and economic history.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Is this the right place?
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Yes, this is the School of Economics.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Oh, good.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Um. I'm a new student, and I was wondering if someone could give me some information.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Well, I might be able to help.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: I lecture on that program.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: What do you need to know?
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Oh, quite a few things actually.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Firstly, how many lectures a week do I have to attend?
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Oh, well, the Economics 1 course is a double unit, so there are two lectures a week and one tutorial.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Oh, the lectures are scheduled for Tuesday and Thursday.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: What time?
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Uh. Let me see.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Um. You know this information is all in the handout which you should have received yesterday at the orientation meeting.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Uh. Oh, was there was there a meeting yesterday?
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: I didn't know about that.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Um. No one yes, ha ha, there was, but never mind.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Now the lectures are at 4 in the afternoon.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Oh, four's a bit late.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: I've got a part-time job that starts at 4:30.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Well, you can't be in two places at once, can you?
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: And attendance at lectures is necessary.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: We expect at least 90% attendance at this university, you know?
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: 90 percent, that's high.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Do they enforce that rule?
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Yes we do.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: We're pretty strict about it actually.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: And what times have been set down for the tutorials?
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Do you have that information?
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: That's a very well attended course, so there's a number of tutorial times, um, Monday, Wednesday and Friday, all at 9:00.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Yours will be allocated at the first lecture.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Can't I choose the time?
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Maybe maybe not, you'll have to talk to the lecturer on the course.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Dr. Roberts is his name.
utterances.speaker: female lecturer

utterances.text: Oh.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: OK.
utterances.speaker: male student

utterances.text: Now look at questions 26 to 31.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items.title: Tutorial paper:

test_text.title: Introduction to the Bachelor of Social Science Degree

audioscript.text: Section 4, you will hear a talk given by a university lecturer about the structure of the university.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, look at questions 32 to 36.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 32 to 36.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good morning, and welcome to the University of Westlands.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Uh. My name is Marcia Mayhew.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: And I'm the coordinator of the Bachelor of Social Science degree.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Uh. This morning, I'd like to tell you about the structure of the university, and s- about some of the requirements of the degree that you're about to enter.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: The Bachelor of Social Science is in one faculty within the university.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: That is the faculty where I work, known as Arts and Social Sciences.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Here on this campus we also have the faculties of Architecture, Law, and Science and Technology among others.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Uh. It's important to know something about the structure of the faculty because as you go through your course, you may need to call on members of the staff to help you.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: At the top of the faculty, we have a dean, and below the dean we have three divisions.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Each division has a divisional head, and your degree is located in the division of Social Sciences.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Within each of the divisions there are the departments, and each of these offers the different degrees.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: For instance, two of the departments which offer the major subjects for your award are sociology and psychology.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Each has a departmental head, but for practical purposes, the people you are going to see the most of are myself as coordinator of the social sciences degree,
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: and the actual lecturers who are teaching the subjects that you are taking.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: For instance, in the first semester, you'll be doing four subjects, psychology, sociology, history and economics.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: If you have any problems or difficulties, uh, not that I'm anticipating you will, but you never know.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Then you should go and see your lecturers.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: For instance, you may find that you can't meet a deadline for an essay, or perhaps you're having problems with attendance.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Uh. These seem to be the two most common problems that students face.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Now look at questions 37 to 41.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: As you listen to the rest of the talk, answer the questions 37 to 41.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: If your lecturers are unavailable, you can always come and see me in my office.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: I'm available on Wednesday and Thursday mornings, and on Friday afternoons.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Outside these hours, uh, perhaps you could ring the secretary and make an appointment.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Now you'll note that all of the subjects which you undertake in the first year are composed of lectures and tutorials.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: A lecture is about an hour long, and a tutorial usually runs for about 2 hours.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: A lecture is rather like what I'm doing now, where one person will talk to all of you together on a subject.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Now, we do ask you to try to attend the lectures.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: A tutorial is perhaps where most of the learning occurs at a university.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: You will be divided into groups of between 12 and 15 students, and each week one of you will have to present a piece of work to the group as a whole.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: And then the group will discuss what you've said.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: It's this discussion, this exchange of ideas which really constitutes the basis of university learning in my view.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Listening to lectures in many ways is just giving you information that you could access for yourself in the library, but the discussion at the tutorial is very important.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: This doesn't mean that you shouldn't go to the lectures though.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Other factors to be particularly concerned about are the structure of essays and delivery of written material.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: And in particular, I would like to mention the question of plagiarism.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Plagiarism is taking other people's work.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Without acknowledging it.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: That is without saying where it comes from.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Now, of course, all essays are based on research done by other people, but you must remember to attribute the work to the original writer.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: And while it's a good idea to work with other people, don't hand in work which is exactly the same as your friends' work, because we will notice.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: If you don't acknowledge the source of your information, then you run the risk of failing the subject or in very serious cases.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: You might be denied entry to the university.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Last but not least, stay in touch with us.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: If things are getting you down, don't go and hide, come and talk to us about it.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: That's what we're here for.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: Um. Thank you very much for coming along today.
audioscript.speaker: Lecturer

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER
